# Artem Personal-OS Pattern — Snowiki Analysis Notes

## Public artifacts
- https://github.com/ArtemXTech
- https://artemxtech.substack.com/p/how-i-manage-10-claude-code-agents
- https://artemxtech.substack.com/p/obsidian-cli-changed-how-my-agent

## What this is
An agent-orchestration and local-workspace operating pattern adjacent to Snowiki's goals. This is more workflow/ergonomics infrastructure than wiki architecture itself.

## Key patterns to preserve for Snowiki

### Session and workspace observability
- named workspaces
- explicit status/context surfaces
- orchestration visibility for multiple agent flows

### Context-efficient local tooling
- CLI-first, local-first ergonomics
- dashboard/status-first operating style

### Human-visible coordination surfaces
- frontmatter/status metadata is part of the operating model

## What Snowiki should treat as contrast material
- This is not a durable wiki-contract source in the same sense as Karpathy or `personal-os-skills`.
- It is better used as an adjacent ergonomics reference than as a primary Step 3 contract source.

## Relevance to Snowiki steps
- Step 3: Wiki skill design (adjacent ergonomics)

## Concrete Snowiki takeaways

1. Status surfaces should remain visible and cheap to inspect.
2. Orchestration context should be explicit rather than hidden in agent memory.
3. Keep this as a supporting note, not a primary architecture anchor.
